howItWorks.intro: This compost c:n ratio calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.

rules.name: Blend C:N
rules.formula: C:N = (Brown lbs × Brown C:N + Green lbs × Green C:N) / (Brown lbs + Green lbs)

howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: What is the ideal C:N ratio?
faq.answer: The ideal range is 25:1 to 35:1 for active composting.

faq.question: What are browns and greens?
faq.answer: Browns are carbon-rich (leaves, cardboard, straw). Greens are nitrogen-rich (food scraps, grass clippings).

faq.question: Is the Compost C:N Ratio Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard gardening and horticulture formulas trusted by gardeners and growers. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What brown material should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate brown material value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

sections.title: What Is the Compost C:N Ratio Calculator?
sections.body: The Compost C:N Ratio Calculator is a free online tool designed for gardeners and growers who need quick, accurate calculations in the gardening and horticulture space. By entering your brown material, brown c:n ratio, green material, you get instant results including blend c:n ratio, rating. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting blend c:n ratio right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In gardening and horticulture, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by gardeners and growers worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to grow a thriving garden with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your brown material and need to find the right blend c:n ratio. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Brown Material (lbs)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your brown material — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: brown c:n ratio, green material, green c:n ratio. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your blend c:n ratio, rating. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your brown material before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both blend c:n ratio and rating — they work together to give you the full picture.

If you're unsure about your green c:n ratio, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
